title: rubus koehleri var. weeberi
intro: Rubus koehleri var. weeberi, also known as the Weeber's wild raspberry or Japanese wild raspberry, is a type of bramble in the rose family, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus koehleri var. weeberi include its ability to help with digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. The plant is often prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or dried extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical applications.
